description:
You are an IT-support technician who has just received a request from your team lead:

        “Please give me a quick CSV export of every currently OPEN ticket that is assigned to Alice.  
         I only need the ticket id, the title, and the priority – nothing else.”

    The company keeps its help-desk data in an SQLite database that is already present on the system:

        /home/user/helpdesk/tickets.db

    Inside that database there is a single table named tickets whose relevant columns are:
        • id        – INTEGER primary key  
        • title     – TEXT  
        • assignee  – TEXT  
        • status    – TEXT (expected values: 'open' or 'closed')  
        • priority  – TEXT  

    Task you must accomplish from the terminal (no GUI tools):

    1. Use the sqlite3 CLI to query the database for all rows where  
           assignee = 'Alice'  AND  status = 'open'.
       You MUST extract only these three columns, in this exact order:
           id, title, priority

    2. Export the query result to a **new** CSV file located at:
           /home/user/helpdesk/alice_open_tickets.csv

       Formatting rules that will be tested:
         • The first line must be a header exactly equal to:  
               id,title,priority
         • Every subsequent line must contain the three comma-separated values with no surrounding
           quotation marks and no extra whitespace.
         • Lines must be separated by a single UNIX newline character (LF, \n).
         • The file must end with a trailing newline after the last record.

    3. Do not modify any other files.  Do not leave the result inside the sqlite3 prompt;
       redirect it into the CSV file.

    Hint: all of this can be done with a single sqlite3 command invocation plus I/O redirection.

    When you are done, verify that:
        • /home/user/helpdesk/alice_open_tickets.csv exists,
        • it is readable for your user,
        • its contents exactly follow the format above.

    No further output is required from the terminal after the CSV is created.
